denial_letter_text:
NOTICE OF ADVERSE BENEFIT DETERMINATION
 Date of Notice: 05/05/2026
Aetna Choice® POS II (employer-sponsored group health plan)
Member ID: W100807738          Group #: GRP-1102
Authorization / Reference #: PA-2026-117995
Claim #: CLM-2026-193407
Date of Service (requested): 04/15/2026
Treating provider (as submitted): Harborview Clinical Partners
 Dear Member,
 This document is notice of an adverse benefit determination regarding prior authorization. We received your provider's prior authorization request on 04/17/2026 for Diagnostic mammogram plus targeted ultrasound related to Breast lump, BI-RADS 0 (N63.0). After clinical review, we are unable to approve this request. CASE SUMMARY
Service requested: Diagnostic mammogram plus targeted ultrasound
Diagnosis (as submitted): Breast lump, BI-RADS 0 (N63.0)
Denial code: CO-197 — Precertification/authorization absent — service requires prior approval before the date of service. Clinical policy applied: CPB 0122 under Aetna Clinical Policy Bulletin (CPB) and InterQual® acute-care criteria
 EXPLANATION OF DECISION
Our review applied the criteria in CPB 0122 and your plan's Summary Plan Description (SPD), Section 4 — Medical Benefits and Utilization Management. Diagnostic mammogram plus targeted ultrasound with an out-of-network provider is not covered without a gap exception. In-network alternatives are available. Specifically, the request does not satisfy the utilization-management requirements associated with out of network no authorization. The requesting provider may submit additional records for consideration on appeal, but the request remains denied at this time. Diagnosis and procedure codes used in this determination are available upon request, including plain-language descriptions, as required by applicable federal standards. If your provider wishes to discuss this determination, they may contact our physician review line. Any discussion must occur within the timeframe described in your plan materials. YOUR RIGHT TO INFORMATION
You may request a copy of your claim file, including documents, clinical criteria, and other information we considered in making this determination. Many members use this information when preparing an appeal. Submit your request to the address shown on this notice or through the member portal identified in your plan materials. APPEAL RIGHTS
You have the right to appeal this determination through our internal appeals process. To file a standard appeal, send a written request and supporting medical records within 180 days of the date of this notice. Include your member identification number (W100807738) and reference PA-2026-117995. You may also have the right to receive, upon request and without charge, copies of documents relevant to this determination. Please note that benefits are subject to all terms, exclusions, and limitations of your group health plan document. This letter constitutes the formal adverse determination for the service identified above. Sincerely,
 Aetna Medical Management
Utilization Management Department
 Standard criteria apply regardless of information previously sent to the plan.
clinical_context: The 19-year-old member has Breast lump, BI-RADS 0 (N63.0). He has been treated under commercial coverage with Diagnostic mammogram plus targeted ultrasound recommended as the next appropriate step. Palpable 1.2 cm mass; age 19; family history breast cancer. Submitted documentation supports medical necessity for Diagnostic mammogram plus targeted ultrasound in the setting of Breast lump, BI-RADS 0 (N63.0); the denial letter does not discuss those records or cite the objective findings in the file.

scenario_realism.reasoning: Diagnosis, prior treatments, and insurer administrative posture reflect everyday commercial UM denials rather than textbook perfection.

insurer_voice.reasoning: Letter reads as authentic Aetna correspondence with CPB and InterQual fingerprints and procedural tone.

financial_auditor.reasoning: Dollar amounts are sparse; denial focuses on medical necessity rather than cost-shifting.

legal_auditor.reasoning: The letter gives a 180-day internal appeal window and claim-file access rights, but it does not mention any external review or IRO right, which correctly expresses the intended missing_iro_notice flaw.
evidence_quotes: You have the right to appeal this determination through our internal appeals process.
To file a standard appeal, send a written request and supporting medical records within 180 days of the date of this notice.

demographic_validator.reasoning: Age and gender in profile align with clinical narrative without stereotype violations.

scope_guard.reasoning: In-scope commercial Aetna Prior Authorization case with no Medicare/Medicaid framing.

date_sanity.reasoning: Referenced dates follow a coherent authorization → denial → appeal window sequence.

citation_traceability.reasoning: The denial cites Aetna CPB 0122 and InterQual acute-care criteria in the generic, edition-free way real insurer denials often do. The citations are vague but plausible rather than hallucinated.

appeal_difficulty.reasoning: Appealability is moderate: the member can attack the missing IRO notice and the denial's failure to engage the submitted physician rationale, but Aetna still has a non-trivial out-of-network/no-authorization defense.
evidence_quotes: Exploitable flaw: denial omits any notice of external review or IRO rights.
Exploitable flaw: denial does not meaningfully engage the physician's submitted rationale or the objective findings in the file.
exploitable_weaknesses: Exploitable flaw: boilerplate ignores prior auth context
Exploitable flaw: makes no reference to submitted evidence
strong_defenses: Denial ties the decision to an out-of-network request without prior authorization or a gap exception.
Letter gives a standard internal appeal window and reserves the right to request more records on appeal.

safety_redactor.reasoning: Commercial-plan denial for an in-scope insurer with no banned-topic language, no violence, and no out-of-scope programs.
